title: trias crassifolia
intro: Trias crassifolia, also known as the White Horsewort or Bitter Clover, is a plant species in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its small, white or pinkish flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, Trias crassifolia has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. The plant is rich in alkaloids, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation. Trias crassifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
